overview: This snake appears consistent with a Pinzón Racer (Pseudalsophis slevini), a species endemic to Pinzón Island in the Galápagos. It shares characteristics with other colubrids, such as a Water False Coral Snake (Hydrops triangularis), which is found in South America. It also resembles a Banded Galapagos Snake, typically featuring a distinct pattern of dark cross-bands. These snakes are generally considered non-venomous to humans, though they are specialized predators of small vertebrates. iNaturalist +2 3 sites Pinzón Racer (Pseudalsophis slevini) · iNaturalist
